response:
The error occurs because WPF assemblies are not available in .NET 7 on macOS and the project is trying to load them even when running on a non-Windows platform.

You need to ensure that WPF references are only included in the project configuration for Windows builds by using conditional compilation or platform-specific project files.

cousin projects often face similar cross-platform challenges when mixing desktop frameworks that are not supported on all operating systems.

Use the Condition attribute in your project file to exclude WPF dependencies when the target framework is not Windows, preventing the assembly load attempt on macOS.

This approach allows your application to remain cross-platform while resolving the file not found error on Windows without downgrading the entire solution to .NET Framework.
